text: Due to their diverse and potent biological profiles, quinazoline derivatives have become a focal point of contemporary synthetic chemistry and pharmacological research. This review provides a comprehensive overview of recent advancements in the synthesis and bioactivity of these N-containing heterocycles. We categorize current synthetic strategies into five primary methodologies: Aza-reactions, microwave-assisted synthesis, metal-mediated processes, ultrasound-promoted reactions, and phase-transfer catalysis. Additionally, the review highlights the therapeutic potential of synthesized quinazoline derivatives, exploring their roles as anti-cancer agents, anti-inflammatory drugs, and antimicrobials, among other medicinal applications. Through this analysis, we aim to provide a valuable reference for the future design and development of more potent quinazoline-based therapeutic agents.
